summary.en-US: Connects the current browser profile to Flow2API and syncs the active profile/container Google Labs session.
summary.zh-CN: 把当前浏览器 Profile 接到 Flow2API，并同步当前页面所属 Profile / 容器的 Google Labs 登录态。

description.en-US: Flow2API Token Updater reads the Google Labs session cookie from labs.google, auto-discovers the plugin connection token from an already logged-in Flow2API console on the same browser after the user authorizes a Flow2API address, and syncs the current browser profile's session to that Flow2API server. Within one Firefox-based profile, the add-on also keeps the last successful Labs session context and recent sync status scoped to the active cookie store/container so different signed-in accounts do not overwrite each other. In the AMO listed build, install-time host access is limited to https://labs.google/*; access to the user-entered Flow2API origin is requested at runtime only when the user connects a site. If a reusable browser login still exists but the related page is not open, the extension may silently open labs.google or the user's Flow2API /manage page in the background to rediscover the session and then close the temporary tab. It supports Chromium browsers and Firefox-based browsers such as Firefox and Zen.
description.zh-CN: Flow2API Token Updater 会从 labs.google 读取当前浏览器 Profile 的登录态；当用户填写 Flow2API 地址并授权访问该域名后，扩展会在同一浏览器内自动读取已登录的 Flow2API 控制台配置，并把当前 Profile 的登录态同步到该 Flow2API 服务。在 Firefox / Zen 的同一个 Profile 内，扩展还会按当前 cookie store / container 分别记住最近一次成功同步所用的 Labs 会话上下文和同步结果，避免不同账号互相覆盖。在 AMO 上架版中，安装时只声明 https://labs.google/* 的 host 权限；用户自己填写的 Flow2API 站点访问权限会在真正连接该站点时再运行时申请。如果浏览器里仍保留可复用的登录态，但对应页面没有打开，扩展可能会在后台静默打开 labs.google 或用户的 Flow2API /manage 页面重新发现会话，并在完成后关闭临时标签页。它支持 Chromium 以及 Firefox / Zen 等 Firefox 系浏览器。

version.approval_notes: This extension accesses cookies for https://labs.google/ to read the __Secure-next-auth.session-token value. After the user enters a Flow2API address and grants access to that site, the extension may read the local admin session state from the user's already logged-in Flow2API console in the same browser in order to auto-discover the plugin connection token. The AMO listed package does not request install-time access to all websites: its install-time host permission is limited to https://labs.google/*, and access to the user-entered Flow2API origin is requested at runtime through optional host permissions only when the user connects a site. When probing for a reusable Flow2API console login, the extension only checks tabs on the user-entered origin whose path starts with /manage or /login, or it opens /manage itself. If a reusable browser login still exists but the related page is not open, the extension may silently open labs.google or the user's Flow2API /manage page in a background tab to rediscover the session and then close the temporary tab. The extracted Google Labs session token is then sent only to the user's own Flow2API endpoint at /api/plugin/update-token. No analytics, advertising, telemetry, or third-party tracking endpoints are included. Local per-profile sync state, the last successful Labs session context used by that browser profile, and logs are stored in browser local storage. In Firefox-based browsers, recent sync state and the remembered Labs session context are further scoped to the active cookie store/container so different signed-in accounts inside one profile do not overwrite each other. Flow2API address and discovered connection token are stored per-profile in browser local storage. If a previously discovered connection token is rejected while the browser still has a reusable Flow2API console login, the extension may silently reopen the user's Flow2API /manage page in a background tab to rediscover the plugin connection token and retry the sync. Privacy policy: https://banana.rematrixed.com/privacy
